title: sebastiania glandulosa var. psilophylla
intro: Sebastiania glandulosa var. psilophylla, also known as the "False Copal" or "False Copahu", is a plant species belonging to the family Phytolaccaceae. It is a shrub or small tree native to the tropical regions of South America, and its resin has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Sebastiania glandulosa var. psilophylla are attributed to its ability to relieve pain, reduce fever, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, making it a popular herbal remedy in folk medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the resin of this plant can be extracted and used in various forms, including infusions, tinctures, and topical ointments, to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
